lucidBlocksBeginnerGuide.title: Clover Origins Codes
lucidBlocksBeginnerGuide.intro: Redeem the current Clover Origins codes for yen, stat resets, and reroll bundles before you settle on a build. The strongest early accounts claim every launch reward first, then use those spins to test multiple grimoire, race, and broom outcomes.

items.bestUse: Redeem this as soon as you create your character so you can test several early grimoire, broom, and race outcomes before committing to one result.
Use this to rebuild after a weak first roll or to push a second wave of rerolls once you understand your preferred playstyle.
Claim it before long quest sessions so early progression is not bottlenecked by low yen or a weak first grimoire roll.
Save this reset until you finish your first levels and know whether your current grimoire is worth building around.

items.code: How to Redeem Clover Origins Codes
rewards: Join the Funzy Labs Roblox group
Enter the code and press Enter
items.bestUse: Redeem every active Clover Origins code before you start serious questing so your spins, yen, and stat resets are ready when the real build choices begin.

highlights: Launch and like-milestone codes are strongest on fresh Clover Origins accounts.
Spend free grimoire, broom, and race spins before you buy anything with yen.
Hold your stat resets until you understand which grimoire and path you want to keep.
Join the Funzy Labs Roblox group first so Clover Origins code redemption works cleanly.

lucidBlocksApotheosisCrafting.title: Clover Origins Beginner Guide
lucidBlocksApotheosisCrafting.intro: The best Clover Origins opening is simple: lock in freebies, quest steadily, unlock your first grimoire, and avoid wasting resets on a build you do not understand yet. Clean progression beats flashy rerolls in the first hour.

steps.label: Pick your side and enter Clover Origins
steps.details: Clover Origins starts with the good-versus-evil split that frames your run as either a Magic Knight or an evil Mage. Make that choice first, then learn the interface before spending anything valuable.

steps.label: Redeem every Clover Origins code immediately
steps.details: Open Menu, tap the ! Info tab, and claim the available rewards. The free yen, stat resets, and reroll bundles remove most of the early pressure from a new account.

steps.label: Quest before rerolling too hard
steps.details: Early Clover Origins progression comes from questing and enemy farming, so get a few levels and learn your movement before burning through every reset and spin.

steps.label: Unlock your first grimoire at the Wizard NPC
steps.details: Once your early levels feel stable, head to the Wizard NPC and take your first real grimoire spin. That is the first build-defining moment on a fresh Clover Origins profile.

steps.label: Keep high-value utility resources in reserve
steps.details: Stat resets are most valuable after you decide whether your current grimoire is worth keeping. Do not burn every reset the moment you receive it.

steps.label: Use yen for progression instead of impulse rerolls
steps.details: Yen supports upgrades tied to your grimoire and better broom options, so spend it on smoother progression instead of random purchases that do not move your account forward.

steps.label: Fold daily and weekly quests into your loop
steps.details: Once your basics are set, daily and weekly quest rhythm becomes the cleanest way to keep Clover Origins progression moving without wasted downtime.

steps.label: Leave PvP for after your build stabilizes
steps.details: PvP exists in Clover Origins, but it makes more sense once you have a grimoire you actually want to keep, a cleaner stat setup, and enough practice with movement and skills.

quickTips: Claim every code before your first long quest session.
Test your grimoire and movement before spending extra stat resets.
Treat yen as progression fuel, not as panic spending money.
A calm first-hour route makes later race, broom, and PvP choices much cleaner.

lucidBlocksToolsAndWeapons.title: Clover Origins Grimoire Tier List
lucidBlocksToolsAndWeapons.intro: The clearest public Clover Origins grimoire pool points to a simple homepage tier list: one true chase roll, two reliable long-term keeps, and two safer progression options that are good enough to build around early.

items.name: Anti Magic
items.summary: Anti Magic is the premium Clover Origins reroll target because the official game materials frame it as the standout rare outcome. If you land it early, it is usually the roll worth building around instead of rerolling away.

items.name: Wind
items.summary: Wind is one of the most reliable public-facing Clover Origins grimoires for general progression. It is a very safe keep if you want a strong early route without chasing the rarest roll forever.

items.name: Fire
items.summary: Fire fits the direct damage fantasy many Clover Origins players want. It is one of the cleanest early keeps if your goal is fast quest clears and aggressive PvE pressure.

items.name: Reinforcement
items.summary: Reinforcement is easy to recommend to Clover Origins players who want steadier fights, more comfort, and less risk while learning the game. It is not the headline chase roll, but it is still a practical early keep.

items.name: Regen
items.summary: Regen works well for calmer solo progression in Clover Origins because it gives new players a more forgiving route through questing and enemy farming. It is dependable even if it is not the top-tier flex.

notes: Anti Magic is the long-term Clover Origins chase roll.
Wind and Fire are the safest early keeps for efficient progression.
Reinforcement and Regen are fine if you value comfort over hype.
Do not spend every reset chasing perfection before you understand your build.

lucidBlocksStorageAndInventory.title: Clover Origins Best Magic Guide
lucidBlocksStorageAndInventory.intro: The best magic in Clover Origins is the one that matches how you plan to clear quests, farm enemies, and decide whether a reroll is worth spending. Pick for playstyle first and rarity second.

items.name: Anti Magic
items.whyPickIt: Anti Magic is the signature Clover Origins chase result because it pairs rarity with top-end identity. If you land it, the usual answer is to keep it and build around it.
items.tradeoff: Tradeoff: you may spend a long time chasing it.

items.name: Fire
items.bestFor: Players who want direct pressure and clean quest clearing
items.whyPickIt: Fire is one of the best Clover Origins magic picks if your goal is straightforward damage and fast early farming without overthinking every rotation.
items.tradeoff: Tradeoff: it is safer than flashy, not the rare prestige roll.

items.name: Wind
items.bestFor: Balanced progression and flexible all-round play
items.whyPickIt: Wind is a strong generalist recommendation because it sits in the visible Clover Origins magic pool and adapts well to both farming and longer-term progression.
items.tradeoff: Tradeoff: reroll hunters may find it less exciting than Anti Magic.

items.name: Regen
items.bestFor: Forgiving solo leveling and sustain-heavy starts
items.whyPickIt: Regen is a very usable Clover Origins magic option when you want a safer, lower-stress start and a bit more room to learn movement and quest pacing.
items.tradeoff: Tradeoff: it is rarely the highest-value roll if peak chase value is all you care about.

items.name: Reinforcement
items.whyPickIt: Reinforcement makes the most sense for Clover Origins players who want to stabilize early fights and learn the game without forcing aggressive damage-first decisions.
items.tradeoff: Tradeoff: it is more of a practical keep than a headline meta flex.

pickingTips: Choose Clover Origins magic by farming comfort and quest speed, not only by rarity.
Landing a practical keep early is better than burning every reset on day one.
Use stat resets only after you know whether your magic and grimoire plan line up.
The right magic is the one you can actually progress with for several sessions.

lucidBlocksQualiaAndBaseBuilding.title: Clover Origins Grimoire Guide
lucidBlocksQualiaAndBaseBuilding.intro: The grimoire system is the heart of Clover Origins because it determines your combat identity, your reroll decisions, and when your stat resets become worth spending. New players should think about grimoires as progression anchors, not just rarity rolls.

cards.name: Unlock Timing
cards.description: Your first Clover Origins grimoire matters most after you have leveled a bit and reached the Wizard NPC. Do not rush into build commitment before that checkpoint.

cards.name: Spin Economy
cards.description: Use your free Clover Origins code rewards first so your initial grimoire decisions come from launch spins instead of wasted yen.

cards.name: Rarity Mindset
cards.description: A dependable grimoire that clears quests well is often better than endless rerolling for a dream result you may not see for hours.

cards.name: Commit Window
cards.description: Stat resets become high value only after your Clover Origins grimoire feels worth keeping. Test the roll first, then commit.

highlights: Treat Anti Magic as the long chase
Reset only after testing your build

lucidBlocksWorldRegions.title: Clover Origins Leveling Guide
lucidBlocksWorldRegions.intro: Fast leveling in Clover Origins comes from stacking quests, moving between efficient enemy packs, and treating build checkpoints like part of the route instead of as distractions. The strongest path is structured, not random.

regions.name: Starter Quest Hub
regions.description: Begin with the closest Clover Origins NPC quests and clear every overlapping objective before moving on. Early quest density matters more than fancy rerolls.

regions.name: Open-World Enemy Loop
regions.description: Stay in areas where quest targets and enemy camps overlap so you gain levels, yen, and familiarity with your movement without extra travel waste.

regions.name: Wizard NPC Checkpoint
regions.description: As soon as your first Clover Origins leveling stretch feels stable, visit the Wizard NPC and secure your first grimoire before returning to longer quest chains.

regions.name: Daily and Weekly Cycle
regions.description: Once Clover Origins dailies and weeklies fit into your account loop, they become one of the cleanest ways to keep progression steady between build decisions.

lucidBlocksCreaturesAndEnemies.title: Clover Origins Stats Guide
lucidBlocksCreaturesAndEnemies.intro: Good Clover Origins stat allocation is about supporting your grimoire, keeping quests smooth, and saving resets for moments when they create real value. Early stat plans should stay practical and reversible.

creatures.name: Primary Damage Stat
creatures.description: Put most early Clover Origins points into the stat that directly supports your grimoire's main damage pattern so your quest clears stay efficient.

creatures.name: Survivability Floor
creatures.description: A small defensive baseline keeps Clover Origins farming cleaner because you spend less time getting punished by basic enemies while learning routes.

creatures.name: Mobility Value
creatures.description: Movement matters in Clover Origins because quest pace and farming efficiency both improve when your repositioning is smoother.

creatures.name: Reset Timing
creatures.description: The best time to use a Clover Origins stat reset is after you decide your grimoire is a keeper, not before you even know what build you want.

creatures.name: PvE First Split
creatures.description: Early Clover Origins stat planning should favor PvE consistency because stable farming and questing create the resources that enable better later builds.

creatures.name: PvP Pivot
creatures.description: Once your Clover Origins build stabilizes, you can start moving stats toward PvP-specific priorities instead of general progression efficiency.

lucidBlocksMobilityGear.title: Clover Origins Race Guide
lucidBlocksMobilityGear.intro: Race spins matter in Clover Origins because they influence long-term account value, reroll pressure, and whether your early resources are being spent efficiently. Race decisions should support your grimoire plan instead of fighting it.

items.name: High-Value Utility Races
items.description: Keep Clover Origins race rolls that immediately strengthen your progression loop instead of rerolling forever for a hypothetical perfect outcome.

items.name: Comfortable Early Keeps
items.description: A solid Clover Origins race that helps you quest smoothly is often more valuable than chasing a better roll before your account is stable.

items.name: Hard Reroll Targets
items.description: Only push aggressive Clover Origins race rerolling if your codes, yen, and grimoire plan can support the extra variance.

items.name: Synergy With Your Grimoire
items.description: Your Clover Origins race should reinforce the strengths of the grimoire you actually plan to keep, not just look good in isolation.

items.name: Spin Discipline
items.description: Save extra Clover Origins race spins when your build still has unanswered questions. Spending every roll immediately creates unnecessary pressure.

unlockMilestones: Spend free Clover Origins race spins first
Reroll only after your grimoire plan is clear
Save extra spins for later updates or pivots

lucidBlocksFarmingAndGrowth.title: Clover Origins Broom Guide
lucidBlocksFarmingAndGrowth.intro: Brooms are Clover Origins mobility progression. They affect travel time, quest efficiency, and how smoothly you can move between combat and NPC checkpoints, so broom spins should be treated as quality-of-life investments with real value.

sections.name: First Broom Goal
sections.description: Your first Clover Origins broom should make route travel cleaner and less annoying. Early comfort matters more than chasing a perfect cosmetic result.

sections.name: Spin Value
sections.description: Free Clover Origins broom spins from codes are the best way to test mobility upgrades without draining yen or sacrificing grimoire progress.

sections.name: Travel Efficiency
sections.description: Better broom movement shortens the distance between Clover Origins quests, NPCs, and farming zones, which adds up fast across long sessions.

sections.name: Upgrade Timing
sections.description: Once your grimoire and stats feel stable, Clover Origins broom improvement becomes one of the easiest ways to increase overall progression comfort.

growthMilestones: Upgrade movement after your build settles
Turn better travel into faster quest loops

lucidBlocksBestEarlyUnlocks.title: Clover Origins Quest Guide
lucidBlocksBestEarlyUnlocks.intro: Questing is the backbone of Clover Origins progression because it drives levels, introduces checkpoints, and creates the cleanest path toward your first real build decisions. Good quest order removes a lot of wasted time.

priorities.name: Main Story Push
priorities.description: The main Clover Origins quest line should stay at the center of your route because it unlocks progression and helps structure where you go next.

priorities.name: Stacked NPC Quests
priorities.description: Take Clover Origins quests that overlap in location and enemy targets so you level, farm, and finish objectives in the same movement loop.

priorities.name: Wizard NPC Timing
priorities.description: Treat the first grimoire unlock as a key Clover Origins quest milestone and fit it into your route as soon as your level is ready.

priorities.name: Daily and Weekly Quests
priorities.description: Once available, Clover Origins dailies and weeklies are efficient repeatable objectives that keep progression moving between build decisions.

priorities.name: Side Objective Cleanup
priorities.description: Finish convenient Clover Origins side quests when they fit your path, but do not let them derail your main leveling and grimoire checkpoints.

priorities.name: PvP Delay
priorities.description: Push Clover Origins questing first and leave PvP for later, when your stats, grimoire, and race choices are no longer experimental.

lucidBlocksAchievementTracker.title: Clover Origins Farming Guide
lucidBlocksAchievementTracker.intro: The best Clover Origins farming routes are repeatable, low-friction, and tied to real build value. Farm for yen, reroll value, and quest efficiency instead of grinding without a target.

achievements.title: Quest stack route
achievements.description: Farm Clover Origins yen through quest chains that overlap enemy camps and travel routes instead of isolated fights.

achievements.title: Stay in efficient zones
achievements.description: The best Clover Origins farming spots are places where movement time is low and objective turn-ins stay close.

achievements.title: Avoid downtime spending
achievements.description: Do not interrupt Clover Origins farming loops with random rerolls every few minutes. Finish a clean route first.

achievements.title: Use free spins first
achievements.description: Clover Origins code rewards should handle your earliest race, broom, and grimoire experiments before you spend extra resources.

achievements.title: Farm after testing
achievements.description: Once your Clover Origins build feels usable, farming becomes easier because your quest clears and enemy loops stay consistent.

achievements.title: Save reroll overflow
achievements.description: If your current Clover Origins build works, saving extra spins can be smarter than chasing another risky pivot.

achievements.title: Pair quests with enemy packs
achievements.description: Clover Origins quest farming is strongest when objectives and combat targets sit in the same loop.

achievements.title: Use broom comfort
achievements.description: A better Clover Origins broom cuts dead time and makes every repeatable route more efficient.

achievements.title: Keep routes compact
achievements.description: Short Clover Origins routes with clean resets beat long paths that waste movement and focus.

achievements.title: Farm for your current build
achievements.description: Target Clover Origins farming loops that support the grimoire and stat setup you actually use, not a hypothetical future build.

achievements.title: Stabilize before PvP
achievements.description: Reliable Clover Origins farming creates the resource floor that makes later PvP pivots safer.

achievements.title: Think session to session
achievements.description: The best Clover Origins farm is the one you can repeat across several sessions without burnout.

lucidBlocksSingleplayerAndPlatformFAQ.title: Clover Origins Best Build Guide
lucidBlocksSingleplayerAndPlatformFAQ.intro: Clover Origins builds only feel strong when grimoire choice, race value, stat allocation, and your preferred farming loop all point in the same direction. Use these answers to keep your build decisions practical.

faqs.question: What is the best beginner build in Clover Origins?
faqs.answer: The best beginner Clover Origins build is one built around a grimoire you can safely keep, supported by practical stats and enough race or broom value to keep questing efficient.

faqs.question: Should a Clover Origins build be chosen before the first grimoire?
faqs.answer: No. In Clover Origins, the first grimoire should heavily influence your build because it tells you whether to commit, reroll, or hold your stat resets.

faqs.question: What makes a Clover Origins build worth keeping?
faqs.answer: A Clover Origins build is worth keeping when it clears quests cleanly, feels comfortable in farming routes, and does not need constant resets to stay playable.

faqs.question: When should a Clover Origins build pivot for PvP?
faqs.answer: Pivot your Clover Origins build toward PvP only after your grimoire, race, broom, and stat foundation feel stable enough that you are not still rebuilding the basics.

faqs.question: How should Clover Origins resets fit into build planning?
faqs.answer: Treat Clover Origins resets as tools for refinement, not panic buttons. They are strongest when you already know the build direction you want to keep.

lucidBlocksSteamDeckAndController.title: Clover Origins Anti Magic Guide
lucidBlocksSteamDeckAndController.intro: Anti Magic is the signature chase outcome in Clover Origins because it combines rarity, identity, and long-term prestige. It is the roll many players benchmark every reroll decision against.

faqs.question: Why is Anti Magic so valuable in Clover Origins?
faqs.answer: Anti Magic is valuable in Clover Origins because it is presented as the standout rare grimoire and represents the highest-profile long-term reroll target.

faqs.question: Should Anti Magic always be kept in Clover Origins?
faqs.answer: In most cases, yes. If you land Anti Magic in Clover Origins, the usual recommendation is to keep it and shape the rest of your build around that result.

faqs.question: Is it worth chasing Anti Magic on a fresh Clover Origins account?
faqs.answer: It can be, but only if you are comfortable with variance. Many Clover Origins players are better served by keeping a strong A-tier grimoire and progressing first.

faqs.question: How should Clover Origins resources be used when chasing Anti Magic?
faqs.answer: Use free code spins first, protect your useful resets, and avoid wrecking a stable Clover Origins account just to force more rerolls than your resources can support.

faqs.question: When should a Clover Origins player stop chasing Anti Magic?
faqs.answer: Stop when your current Clover Origins grimoire clears content well and the next reroll wave would cost more progress than it is likely to gain.

lucidBlocksSettingsAndAccessibility.title: Clover Origins PvP Guide
lucidBlocksSettingsAndAccessibility.intro: PvP in Clover Origins rewards players who bring a stable build, clean movement, and realistic expectations about where their account stands. Arena success starts before the duel opens.

settings.name: Build Stability First
settings.description: Bring a Clover Origins build you actually plan to keep. PvP is much harder when your stats and grimoire are still experimental.

settings.name: Know Your Range
settings.description: The best Clover Origins PvP openers come from understanding your magic's range, pressure windows, and escape options before the fight starts.

settings.name: Mobility Matters
settings.description: Good Clover Origins movement creates more wins than random aggression because spacing, dodges, and chase control decide a lot of fights.

settings.name: Reset for Purpose
settings.description: Save Clover Origins stat resets for real PvP refinement after your PvE route is already stable, not before.

lucidBlocksUpdatesAndPatchNotes.title: Clover Origins Good and Evil Path Guide
lucidBlocksUpdatesAndPatchNotes.intro: The good-versus-evil split is one of the clearest identity hooks in Clover Origins. Your path should fit the fantasy you want to live with for quests, build flavor, and later PvP identity.

entries.title: Choose Your Side Early
entries.description: Clover Origins asks you to frame your run as a Magic Knight or an evil Mage right away, so pick the fantasy you are actually interested in keeping.

entries.title: Let Quests Teach the Path
entries.description: Your Clover Origins side choice should feel natural in the quest route you are following, not like a random selection detached from progression.

entries.title: Match Your Build Identity
entries.description: Good and evil choices in Clover Origins work best when your grimoire, stats, and race decisions reinforce the same overall identity.

entries.title: Use PvP as a Late Expression
entries.description: Once your Clover Origins build stabilizes, your chosen path becomes part of how you present yourself in PvP and community discussion.

lucidBlocksCrashFixAndTroubleshooting.title: Clover Origins First Grimoire Guide
lucidBlocksCrashFixAndTroubleshooting.intro: Your first grimoire unlock is the first real build checkpoint in Clover Origins. If you handle that moment well, the rest of your early progression becomes much easier to manage.

steps.title: Reach the Wizard NPC
steps.description: Quest through the opening Clover Origins route until you are ready to visit the Wizard NPC. Do not treat the first grimoire like a random side activity.

steps.title: Spend Free Spins Before Buying More
steps.description: Use your launch and like-milestone Clover Origins rewards first so your initial grimoire evaluation comes from free resources.

steps.title: Judge the Roll by Progression Value
steps.description: Ask whether your first Clover Origins grimoire feels good in quests and farming. A practical keeper is often better than endless chasing.

steps.title: Pair the Roll With Smart Stats
steps.description: Once the grimoire looks usable, tune your Clover Origins stats to support it instead of immediately rerolling into uncertainty.

steps.title: Commit or Reset With Intention
steps.description: If the roll is worth keeping, build around it. If not, use Clover Origins resets and extra spins in a controlled way rather than panicking through every resource at once.

faq.title: Clover Origins
faq.subtitle: Quick answers on Clover Origins codes, grimoires, quests, races, brooms, and build planning.

questions.question: How do I redeem Clover Origins codes?
questions.answer: Open Clover Origins, enter the Menu, tap the ! Info tab, and submit each active code after joining the Funzy Labs Roblox group.

questions.question: When do I get my first grimoire in Clover Origins?
questions.answer: Your first Clover Origins grimoire comes after the early questing stretch when you reach the Wizard NPC and use your first grimoire spin.

questions.question: What is the best grimoire to keep in Clover Origins?
questions.answer: Anti Magic is the top Clover Origins chase roll, while Wind and Fire are the safest strong keeps for practical early progression.

questions.question: Should I spend Clover Origins stat resets immediately?
questions.answer: No. Clover Origins stat resets are strongest after you test your grimoire and decide whether the build is worth keeping.

questions.question: Are Clover Origins race and broom spins worth saving?
questions.answer: Yes. Free Clover Origins race and broom spins are best used first, but extra spins should be saved when your build direction is still undecided.

questions.question: When should I start PvP in Clover Origins?
questions.answer: Start Clover Origins PvP after your grimoire, stats, and movement feel stable enough that you are not still rebuilding the foundation of your account.

biomes.whatIsDescription: Guides to different world regions, environmental features, resource distribution, and danger zones. Learn what to expect and how to prepare for each area.

whySteps.step1Description: Locate rare materials and essences specific to each region
whySteps.step2Description: Understand environmental hazards and hostile creatures
whySteps.step3Description: Prepare the right tools and gear for each biome type

building.title: Lucid Blocks Building
building.description: Master Qualia personal dimensions, base building, storage management, and creative construction techniques.
building.metaTitle: Lucid Blocks Building - Qualia & Base Construction
building.metaDescription: Master Lucid Blocks building with Qualia personal dimensions, base construction, storage solutions, and creative building techniques.
building.whatIsDescription: Guides to the Qualia personal dimension system, base construction, storage management, and creative building. Learn to create your safe haven in the surreal world.

whySteps.step1Description: Establish secure bases for storage and crafting operations
whySteps.step2Description: Organize materials efficiently with storage solutions
whySteps.step3Description: Access personal pocket dimensions for ultimate convenience

support.title: Lucid Blocks Support
support.description: Technical support, platform compatibility, performance optimization, and troubleshooting for common issues.
support.metaTitle: Lucid Blocks Support - Technical Help & Fixes
support.metaDescription: Get technical support for Lucid Blocks including Steam Deck setup, crash fixes, performance optimization, and platform compatibility guides.
support.whatIsDescription: Technical guides covering platform compatibility, performance optimization, crash fixes, and common issues. Get help with Steam Deck, multiplayer questions, and settings.

whySteps.step1Description: Resolve crashes, performance problems, and compatibility issues
whySteps.step2Description: Configure settings for best experience on your platform
whySteps.step3Description: Learn about Steam Deck, controller support, and system requirements
